completed_tasks.name: Repair GSD planning state and capture the current fullscreen presentation baseline

remaining_tasks.name: Redesign Phase 13 motion/art direction to match the scraped reference quality bar
Verify the upgraded presentation against the Phase 13 success criteria and only then mark complete

blockers.description: Current Phase 13 implementation is only a working fullscreen baseline. User explicitly said the animations are nowhere near thorough enough or Awwwards-level enough.
blockers.workaround: Use the existing fullscreen implementation as a foundation, then push the motion system, scene composition, and reference fidelity much further before re-marking the phase complete.

human_actions_pending.action: Use the scraped/reference websites as the acceptance bar for Phase 13 polish decisions
human_actions_pending.context: The user explicitly rejected treating the current GSAP pass as finished.

decisions.decision: Phase 13 must remain open even though a fullscreen presentation implementation exists
decisions.rationale: Working fullscreen behavior is not the same as meeting the intended Awwwards-level quality bar

decisions.decision: Keep the current fullscreen code and phase artifacts as the baseline instead of deleting them
decisions.rationale: They provide a concrete starting point for the real completion pass

next_action: Resume on Phase 13 by reading website/presentation.js, website/style.css, and the phase 13 planning files, then upgrade the motion/art direction to the scraped-reference quality bar before touching phase completion metadata again.
context_notes: This session repaired broken GSD roadmap/state metadata, briefly marked Phase 13 complete based on existing code, then reversed that after the user clarified that the current fullscreen implementation is far below the intended finish quality. The right mental model is: Phase 13 has a functional baseline, not a finished presentation. Do not treat fullscreen + navigation as success. The next session should stay focused on cinematic quality, scene treatment, and reference fidelity.
